posts.caption:
Meet Kafeel Ahmed Ansari aka @painterkafeelartist, the Old Dehli sign painter bringing hand-painted heat to Fred Again..’s viral India tour poster.⁠
⁠
Kafeel is among the last master sign painters and has spent decades perfecting his craft. The acrylic-on-canvas poster is built around the producer’s “again” moniker, stacked in Hindi, Marathi, and Kannada, complete with floral motifs and intricate scrollwork, tapping into Indian truck and street art traditions.⁠
⁠
Video: Gallery XXL (@xxl.gallery)⁠
Photo: Akshat Nauriyal
Hand-painted by Painter Kafeel (@painterkafeelartist )⁠
Design and Composition by Pragyesh (@rerioz)⁠ in the HandPaintedType style (@handpaintedtype)
Artwork featured:
Proverbs Project 2017, Hanif Kureshi x Painter Kaleef (Supported by Serendipity Arts Festival)
Donate A Wall, 2017, Painter Kaleef and Hanif Kureshi for Asian Paints

posts.caption:
@ed_curtis__ is pushing textile experimentation to new limits for his @evianwater collab.
Along with two fellow British artists, the mixed-media creative was enlisted to design a Wimbledon-ready bottle bag in celebration of the brand's 200th anniversary, and as expected, he didn't hold back.
Through harnessing his latest process with fabric and injecting an innate childlike wonder, Curtis crafted a boldly layered masterpiece with second-hand swatches cut into strawberry shapes and tennis ball motifs that frame the project's “200 Years Young” tagline.
To find out more about the project, check out out the latest installment of 'Hypebeast Visits.'
The exclusive bottle bag is now available on evian®'s website where fans can sign up to get one of 200 pieces through entering a waitlist. T&Cs apply.
In partnership with evian®

posts.alt_text:
Un rovescio a una mano, raro nel tennis di oggi, e un gioco elegante e variato: @Lore_Musetti, 24 anni, ha aperto il 2026 conquistando il quinto posto del ranking ATP, prima che un infortunio alla gamba sinistra lo costringesse a rinunciare al Roland Garros. Global ambassador di #BottegaVeneta, il tennista si racconta a @LOfficielItalia, ritratto dagli scatti di @RaffGrosso e con lo styling di @Fernando.Echeverria_, vestendo un total look della collezione #Fall2026, firmato dalla direttrice creativa @Louise_Trotter_. Tra scaramanzie da spogliatoio, il bronzo olimpico di Parigi, l'ammirazione per il tennis di Alcaraz e la passione per la cucina: «Colpire la pallina, il suono che fa quando la colpisco: è questo che mi rende felice». Leggi l’intervista a cura di @Fabia.DiDrusco al link in bio e sul nuovo numero de @LOfficielItalia, ora in edicola.

@lore_musetti
Global Chief Content Officer @giampietrobaudo

posts.caption:
Introducing our global cover star, @TravisScott!
The Houston-born artist has spent the past year moving between worlds, crafting the follow-up to 2023’s ‘Utopia’ from his Malibu studio while preparing for his much-anticipated acting debut as a bard in Christopher Nolan’s @theodysseymovie, in theaters July 17.
From music to film to fashion, Scott’s creative universe continues to expand. He also stars alongside Owen Wilson in the upcoming comedy ‘Rolling Loud’ and maintains long-running partnerships with @Nike and @Oakley, all while leading @cactusjack into its next chapter.
Yet at the center of it all remains the music. “I’ve been mad inspired over the last year and a half,” Scott says. “I’ve been buckled down and back building beats from scratch, just creating and pushing the sound.”
Read our full cover story at the link in bio as Scott opens up about the creative forces that drive him, his big-screen debut, and the importance of self-care.

posts.caption:
«La vera Niña è me stessa bambina. È stata veramente eroica». Nella voce di @LaNinaDelSud convivono memoria e futuro: la musica pre-romantica incontra l’elettronica più viscerale, mentre un’attitudine punk attraversa ogni sua scelta. Dopo il viaggio di “Furèsta”, l’artista campana apre un nuovo capitolo con “RÀRECHE”, il nuovo brano disponibile su tutte le piattaforme digitali che inaugura il percorso con Sugar Music e conferma il suo linguaggio personale sospeso tra tradizione, sperimentazione e identità.
Continua a leggere l’intervista a cura di @FabiaDiDrusco al link in bio.

posts.caption:
His work has brought him to countless destinations across the globe, but @TravisScott still has one major stop left on his bucket list. “I hit every continent on the last tour except Antarctica,” he says. “It’s kind of crazy.”
For Scott, travel is about more than seeing new places—it’s about expanding perspective, and he encourages leaning into exploration whenever possible. “Go touch earth and go actually interact with this world,” he says. “It could change your mindset.”
Read more about Scott’s adventures at the link in our bio.

posts.caption:
The 2026 July/August issue is here!
At the center of this year’s Summer Entertainment Issue is @TravisScott, who opens up about working on the follow-up to 2023’s ‘Utopia,’ the travels and experiences fueling his next chapter, and making his acting debut as part of a star-studded ensemble in Christopher Nolan’s ‘The Odyssey.’
As much as we love a blockbuster with a stacked A-list cast, championing fresh talent is always top of mind. Inside the issue, we spotlight eight rising stars whose work this summer demands your attention, from ‘Outer Banks’ star @MadisonBaileyBabe to Avantika of ‘Not Suitable for Work’ to @sashacalle of ‘Sugar.’
It’s also a blockbuster time for literary escapes, as the rise of #BookTok turns reading into an unexpected status symbol that’s shaping summer travel. Looking ahead to the Fall collections, we explore the age-old question of whether sex still sells, given the state of undress on the runway, red carpet, and social feeds in 2026. Stay tuned for the issue hitting newsstands this month.

posts.caption:
@lilycollias didn’t know what to expect after auditioning for AppleTV’s limited-series remake of ‘Cape Fear.’ “I felt like I could confidently walk away from it knowing I put my best foot forward,” she tells L’OFFICIEL. Then came the call from her agent, asking how she would feel about working with Amy Adams, Javier Bardem, and Patrick Wilson. “I was like, ‘Ummm, I guess I feel pretty good?’”
When Collias—fresh off her breakout performance in 2024’s ‘Good One’—learned she would be stepping into the same role that earned Juliette Lewis an Academy Award nomination in 1991, the pressure quickly set in. “When I think of Juliette Lewis, I think of the ultimate rock star,” says Collias. “I didn’t realize ‘Cape Fear’ was what put her into that place. It’s overwhelming to think about. I was like, ‘How do I even try to do this?’”
With the help of a pivotal bang trim—and guidance from Lewis herself—Collias ultimately made the role her own. At the link in bio, she opens up about her love of darker storytelling, her Hollywood mentors, and what’s next, including summer travel plans with ‘The Young People’ co-stars Lola Tung and Nico Parker.
Editor-in-Chief of @lofficielusa @caroline_grosso⁠

posts.caption:
For @SashaCalle, creativity comes in many forms. From starring opposite Colin Farrell in Season 2 of Apple TV’s ‘Sugar’ to pursuing her passion for songwriting, the actor is embracing every side of her artistry—including stepping into uncharted territory with her first horror role in a new installment of ‘The Exorcist’ franchise, directed by Mike Flanagan and co-starring Scarlett Johansson, Diane Lane, and Chiwetel Ejiofor.
“I’m a scaredy-cat and had never done horror before, so I was skeptical,” she tells L’OFFICIEL. “But when I met Mike, I just adored him. He really saw me, and that is my dream. He also does Labubu Fridays, which brings everyone so much joy.”
After years of working to make her dreams a reality, Calle knows what it means to persevere—a quality she shares with her ‘Sugar’ character, Gina. “I know that feeling where it seems like all the odds are against you, and you have to figure it out,” she says. “That hustler mentality can be really relatable.” Read more of our conversation with Calle at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
For our July/August issue, we caught up with Avantika to discuss her first leading TV role: portraying a celebrity stylist’s assistant in Hulu’s New York–centric sitcom ‘Not Suitable for Work.’ The experience was both daunting and deeply relatable. “So much of life in the city—especially as a young person—is hustle culture,” she tells L’OFFICIEL. “There are many opportunities for love and romance, but there’s also this feeling that you have to compromise or sacrifice those things in order to advance your career. Navigating the push and pull of work-life balance is not inherently very sexy. But I find it exciting.”
Living with what she calls “radical optimism,” the 21-year-old—who built a career in Indian cinema before her American film debut in ‘Spin’ in 2021—is embracing her Hollywood chapter. “I used to dream of days like this,” she says. “I think most young women who come from a very rich culture and then move somewhere like America are told that there are very clear parameters for what they can and can’t be. I hope that, through the characters that I play, young Indian girls see a real and emboldening representation of women who go beyond that.”
Read more of our conversation with Avantika at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
@Balenciaga dropped its Spring Summer 2026 preview collection in Mumbai last night, and the energy in the room felt different.
With Fatima Sana Sheikh, Bobby Deol, Mouni Roy, Sanya Malhotra, Malavika Mohanan, and Krithi Shetty in attendance, it felt less like a formal showcase and more like a real moment around fashion. The room was sharp, observant, and engaged.
Fatima Sana Shaikh said it best: “Balenciaga always has a chic approach and the brand is now heading towards it even more aggressively.” And that tone carried through the evening. Nothing felt loud for attention, yet everything commanded it.
What stood out was how people responded. There was curiosity, closer inspection, and genuine interest in the details. Some silhouettes felt familiar but more refined, while others brought a quiet sense of drama without trying too hard.
That is when a collection works.
Not when it demands attention, but when it earns it.
@balenciaga #Balenciaga

posts.caption:
Bentley has officially revealed the name of its first fully electric model: the Torcal.
Inspired by the dramatic limestone landscapes of El Torcal de Antequera in southern Spain, the new name continues Bentley’s tradition of drawing inspiration from iconic natural landmarks. Scheduled to make its global debut on September 23, the Torcal will be a luxury electric SUV that marks the beginning of a new era for the British marque.
Expected to offer over 300 miles (480+ km) of range, cutting-edge 800-volt architecture, and Bentley’s signature craftsmanship, the Torcal represents the brand’s vision for electrified luxury while staying true to its heritage of performance, refinement, and bespoke design.
@BentleyMotors #Bentley #Luxurious

posts.caption:
Hennessey has unveiled the Venom F5-M, officially claiming the title of the world’s most powerful manual hypercar.
At its heart is a 6.6-litre twin-turbocharged “Fury” V8 producing an astonishing 2,031hp, paired with a six-speed gated manual transmission that sends all the power to the rear wheels. Built on the upgraded Venom F5 Evolution platform, the hypercar features revised aerodynamics, active suspension, a bespoke carbon-fibre body, and an open-top roadster design focused on delivering the ultimate driver experience. Only 12 examples will be produced worldwide, with prices starting at $2.65 million.
Making its production debut at the Goodwood Festival of Speed, the Venom F5-M is a bold statement that, even in an era of electrification and paddle shifters, there is still a place for raw power and the thrill of a manual gearbox.
@hennesseyspecialvehicles @hennesseyperformance

What stood out was how people responded. There was curiosity, closer inspection, and genuine interest in the details. Some silhouettes felt familiar but more refined, while others brought a quiet sense of drama without trying too hard.

That is when a collection works.
Not when it demands attention, but when it earns it. 

@balenciaga #Balenciaga

posts.caption:
Chanel’s Fall/Winter 2026 Haute Couture collection brings classic fairy tales to life through extraordinary craftsmanship.
Inspired by Jack and the Beanstalk, the collection transforms the story’s magical beanstalk into couture, with intricate guipure, transparent silk jacquard, delicate mousseline, and nature-inspired embroidery symbolizing growth and transformation. Every look reflects the House’s ability to turn imagination into haute couture through exceptional hand craftsmanship.
With fairy tales hidden in every detail, Chanel once again proves that couture is as much about storytelling as it is about fashion.
@ChanelOfficial #Chanel #Luxurious

posts.caption:
Max Verstappen’s future at Red Bull has taken another dramatic turn.
Following the British Grand Prix, the four-time Formula 1 world champion can now officially trigger a performance-related exit clause in his contract. With only two races remaining before the summer break, Verstappen can no longer mathematically climb into the top two of the Drivers’ Championship, activating a clause that could allow him to leave Red Bull ahead of the 2027 season.
While Verstappen remains under contract until the end of 2028, the latest development has intensified speculation over his future, with Mercedes and McLaren continuing to be linked as potential destinations. If he chooses to activate the clause, it could lead to one of the biggest driver moves in modern Formula 1.
#Luxurious

posts.caption:
A reader asked our fashion critic: "I understand that we’re all dressing more casually these days, especially so since the pandemic. But there is something special about going to a play or a nice restaurant, and increasingly I’m seeing folks at such places dressed in what they’d wear to the supermarket or to do chores around the house — which kind of makes me sad. Are we losing something?"
The rising casualness of the world is making us reconsider what getting “dressed up” means. Is that a bad thing? At the link in our bio, read @vvfriedman's answer. Photo by Jeremy Moeller/Getty Images
posts.alt_text:
At Chanel's couture show on Tuesday in Paris, the designer Matthieu Blazy went beyond the super sucked-in, laced-up styles of the moment.

Since his debut for the brand last October, Blazy has been offering surprising, almost subversive collections designed to liberate the body beneath, rather than constrict. Amid the the recent dominance of the GLP-1 body, style has evolved to meet the demand for tinier clothing — waists snatched, skirts hobble-tight — with mermaid silhouettes and corset dresses ruling red carpets and runways.

Instead, Chanel offered storytelling, and space to move. Fairy tales were the inspiration for his collection; tales of childhood woven into the clothes on the runway. The airy shift dresses, mousseline skirts with pockets and even the tailored tweed looks were clearly made for movement. At the link in our bio, see more photos from @chanelofficial's couture collection and read @vvfriedman's full review. Photos by @simbarashecha

posts.caption:
At the Dior couture show on Monday in Paris, there was an elephant the room: the still-secret design of the Dior wedding dress Taylor Swift had worn a mere three days earlier.
Was it more classic — i.e., cliché — Dior, or the more current, somewhat challenging Dior? Was it princess-y? Prom-like? Kinda conceptual? Was it, in other words, more like the image that Swift has been presenting to the world, at least onstage, or more like the one that the newish designer Jonathan Anderson has been building for the brand?
The show didn’t necessarily offer any clues. But it did demonstrate why Swift might have thought Dior was the right brand for her in the first place. Anderson's work is "increasingly both notably intricate and apparently relaxed, a tough balancing act to pull off," wrote Vanessa Friedman, chief fashion critic for The New York Times. At the link in our bio, see more photos from @dior's couture show and read @vvfriedman's full review. Photos by Emma Da Silva/@apnews, @simbarashecha

posts.caption:
Introducing the most stylish people of 2026, so far. As we reflect on the past six months of style, our list of stylish people is not merely an appreciation of really great clothes, though there are plenty of those. It is also a snapshot of pop culture, viral moments and figures who commanded the public’s attention (and dressed the part).
Some ignited discourse, and discord, with a single look. Others used fashion to manifest some element of their identity or as an exuberant expression of their personality.
The great fashion writer Kennedy Fraser once argued that style is a moving target; it is also “individualistic” and “reckless,” she said. Some looks may not be for you, but that’s the point. At the link in our bio, see the full list of the most stylish people so far this year. Photos by Matt Slocum/@apnews, The Hapa Blonde/GC Images, Jeopardy Productions, @poupayphoto, @vincentalban1, @vnina, Denis Balibouse/Reuters, TheStewartofNY/GC Images, Mario Anzuoni/Reuters, Cameron Browne/NBAE, via Getty Images, Scott A Garfitt/Invision, via @apnews

posts.caption:
The nude or minimal nail aesthetic is everywhere in 2026, from runways to street wear and even a “Love Story” plotline. Nail salons and polish brands have reported growing preferences for nude and pink polish, while some people are skipping polish altogether. For some, the trend has become a rallying cry against complex beauty rituals. For others, it’s a way for the privileged few to show their wealth.
The look has become a status symbol, for everyone from influencers like Alix Earle, Paige Lorenze and Tinx, who all wore it in recent weeks; to perennial it girls like Zoë Kravitz and the Olsen twins; to Marc Jacobs, who sent models down a runway this year with nude nails at New York Fashion Week. When did the trend start and why? Tap the link in our bio to read more about why bare nails are everywhere. Photo by Stephen Lovekin/Shutterstock

posts.caption:
Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce married in an elaborately planned wedding with garden themes and plenty of personalized “T&T” logo details. Both were wearing Dior, though pictures have not been released yet.
Adam Sandler officiated a ceremony where the couple read their own vows. The couple’s brothers stood by their sides as the man of honor and best man.
Though few details have been confirmed by the couple, a picture was beginning to emerge of what the most star-studded night in New York in recent memory looked like. What was the theme of the décor? What was on the menu? At the link in our bio, read what we know about @taylorswift and @killatrav's wedding. Photo by @poupayphoto

posts.caption:
#ManifestCover
There is a quiet power to Rukmini Vasanth (@rukmini_vasanth). One that doesn’t seek attention, yet holds it effortlessly.
For Manifest’s May–June cover, she steps into an imaginative world where temple jewellery meets contemporary silhouettes, blurring the lines between memory and modernity. She embodies a woman in transition, grounded, luminous, and yet constantly becoming.
“I’m not very good at describing myself,” she says, almost in passing.
Yet, in the same breath, her choices reveal a quiet clarity of intent. “I don’t want to isolate my character. I need to understand the whole world it exists in.”
Between restraint and reinvention, stillness and movement, she exists in that rare in-between. Not fully defined, not confined. Just on the cusp of a wider sky.
Our latest issue is now live on Magzter. Read the full cover story from the link in bio.
Chairperson: Avarna Jain (@avarna_jain)
CEO: Debashish Ghosh
Editor: Aindrila Mitra (@aindrilamitra)
Interview: Akshay Kaushal (@akshay_kaushal)
Photography: Vaishnav Praveen (@vaishnavpraveen)
Styling: Mohit Rai (@mohitrai)
Fashion assistant: Shubhi Kumar (@shubhi.kumar)
Hair: Rohit Bhatkar (@rohit_bhatkar)
Makeup: Riviera Lynn (@rivieralynn)
Video: Kris Arshad (@itskrisarshad)
PR Agency: Tree-Shul Media (@treeshulmediasolutions)
Artist management: Benchmark Talents (@benchmarktalents)
Production: Studio Little Dumpling (@studiolittledumpling)
On Rukmini: pantsuit, Kanika Goyal Label (@kanikagoyallabel); earrings, bangles, and rings, Gehna Jewellers (@gehnajewellers1)

#Manifest #manifestcover #rukminivasanth
posts.alt_text:
When it comes to Indian weddings, there's an accessory that's part of every girl's ethnic ensemble. A lil pair of juttis that transitions from haldi afternoons to mehendi soirees, qawwali nights and main day rituals, too! And Fizzy Goblet's (@fizzygoblet) pretty juttis with their embellishments, threadwork and new silhouttes, have been dropping all the right swag. Founder and creative head Laksheeta Govil (@laksheeta) has whipped up a contemporary vibe for this footwear classic, pushing the envelope and loving every minute of it!

In her universe, neons go into a cool meltdown, metallics play elegant muse and florals pack all the right charm. Mules, heels, ballet flats and sneakers, added to the mix. And now, Bollywood star Kareena Kapoor Khan (@kareenakapoorkhan)-who's shared a long-time association with the brand-steps in as investor, deepening their partnership. We chat with Laksheeta on why treading new ground makes for so much fun.

Read the full story via the link in bio🔗
By Ismat Tahseen (@ismat.tahseen)

——

Manifest Approved: An interview with the people shaping the wedding industry, from designers to planners and cultural tastemakers. Each story explores the business, craft and evolving expectations behind modern weddings.

posts.caption:
Wedding invitations in 2026 are no longer just about announcing a date—they’re setting the tone for the entire celebration. Couples are embracing a blend of minimal aesthetics, immersive wedding websites, meaningful storytelling, and personalised details to create invitations.
Clearly, it’s a shift from traditional, and overly-decorative designs. The new vibe? Sculpted embossing and nostalgic Indian motifs that feature the beloved genda phool to destination-inspired artworks!
While digital platforms are making the guest experience smoother through custom websites and seamless RSVPs, these nouveau print invitations seem to be grabbing their own spotlight.
Read more via the link in bio🔗
By Srishti Mukherjee (@sri.sshhhhh)
[Wedding Invitation Trends, Shift in Trends 2026, Design and Aesthetic, Quirky Invites]

posts.caption:
If you’ve been on a wedding jewellery buying spree you know the drill—it’s an exercise fraught with visiting spiffy places that work on silence, nods, smiles and quiet conversation. No place for fun, let alone dining while looking at jewellery busts and necklace displays. But Tiara Dhody’s (@tiaradhody) new space Treasures Cafe, on Peddar Road, is breaking that mould with a refreshing narrative—it blends the jewellery shopping with food, fun and friendly banter.
Come in, browse around and tuck into her family recipes, served with a side of jewellery. Who wouldn’t like a little happy bling over a meal!
📍Treasures Cafe, Mumbai
By Ismat Tahseen (@ismat.tahseen)
Link in bio🔗
—
Manifest Check-In: A curated walkthrough of luxury and boutique spaces, exploring the aesthetics, functionality and experiences that define exceptional venues. Featuring restaurants, hotels, wedding venues and unique event destinations.

posts.caption:
Gen Z is making sure nobody’s just standing around waiting for the next function at an Indian wedding.
We’re talking cricket tournaments, DIY perfume bars, IV drip lounges, cocktails served in syringes, dancing gorilla mascots, sunset pool parties and underground-style music nights. And that’s just the beginning.
Gen Z doesn’t want a wedding they simply attend—they want one they can play, film, post and remember. The vibe? Less “please take your seats,” more “everyone’s having fun.”
Read the full story via the link in bio. 🔗
By Kratagya Rathore (@kratagya_rathore)
Image credits: Camlition Productions (@camlition_productions), The Insta Shaadi (@insta_shaadi)

posts.caption:
India's Renee Noronha has won first place in the 18-24 category at the Ironman Philippines. Completing the gruelling challenge at 15:55:37, the 20-year-old IIT Madras student upheld held her record of being the youngest Indian female Ironman finisher.
Renee battled torrential rains and unforgiving temperatures to complete the race. In conversation with SheThePeople, the triathlete also revealed that she participated on day 3 of her period, making the victory a profound reminder of her strength and resilience as a woman.
With this win, Renee has now qualified for the Ironman World Championship Hawaii, scheduled for October 2026. She is also on a mission to set a new Guinness world record to become the youngest person in the world to do a full Ironman on 6 continents. She is on track to achieve this by April 2027, at the age of 21.

India's Renee Noronha has won first place in the 18-24 category at the Ironman Philippines. Completing the gruelling challenge at 15:55:37, the 20-year-old IIT Madras student upheld held her record of being the youngest Indian female Ironman finisher.

Renee battled torrential rains and unforgiving temperatures to complete the race. In conversation with SheThePeople, the triathlete also revealed that she participated on day 3 of her period, making the victory a profound reminder of her strength and resilience as a woman.

With this win, Renee has now qualified for the Ironman World Championship Hawaii, scheduled for October 2026. She is also on a mission to set a new Guinness world record to become the youngest person in the world to do a full Ironman on 6 continents. She is on track to achieve this by April 2027, at the age of 21.

Read full interview at www.shethepeople.tv. | @renee.noronha

posts.caption:
The perfect way to unwind after a long day? Light a candle. Make some tea. Curl up with a good book, let the words wash over you, drift into something quiet and nourishing.
No. Open Instagram. Scroll until the candle burns out. Somewhere along the way, this became the unwind.
The most convenient explanation for this is that we’ve all rotted. The Instagram algorithm has hijacked our dopamine. Our attention spans have shrunk to the length of a YouTube Short. And us? Passive recipients of whatever the feed decides we deserve.

posts.caption:
When a woman decides to chase her dreams in India, loads of hurdles await her on her way. Be it society’s conservatism, stereotypes, or even families who preserve old misogynistic traditions. Hina Bano did not let her religion win over her ambitions, and she is now part of the Indian senior women's hockey team.
At the age of two, Hina lost her father. She was raised by her maternal grandfather, who was completely against women playing sports. Hina suppressed her passion for hockey until she met her coach, who encouraged her to play sports.
In a conversation with Shethepeople, Hina Bano talks about choosing sports against her family’s wish and shares how she keeps herself motivated on tough days.
Visit www.shethepeople.tv to read the full interview by @_shivanilamba

posts.caption:
Mehek and Sahil met on a dating app and engaged in heavy ‘love bombing’ even before their second date. Suddenly, everything stopped, and he became cold and distant, leading them into a ‘situation-ship’. Mehek realised he was ‘benching’ her and chatting with other women on the same app. Sahil was ‘bread-crumbing’ her just to keep her hooked. When Mehek finally asked for clarity on where they stood, Sahil didn't respond and ‘ghosted’ her. When did love turn into a confusing vocabulary test?
From ‘love’ and ‘marriage’, ‘break up’ and ‘divorce’ to ‘open relationships’ and ‘live-in relationships’ to Gen Z terms like ghosting, situation-ships, benching, etc., relationship terms have evolved in the age of dating apps and social media. Dating in this age is more about labels.
Visit www.shethepeople.tv to read more

posts.caption:
@janhvikapoor walked into her sister Anshula’s pheras in a custom Anamika Khanna lehenga that felt less like couture and more like a soft, golden secret — ivory-on-ivory, hand-embroidered, lit like something out of an old family photograph nobody’s seen yet.
The blouse alone could stop a room — resham and zari florals climbing across a deep-V, scalloped edges instead of a clean hem, like it was finished by hand and not by design. The dupatta wasn’t pinned into place. It was left to fall, loose over one arm, moving with her instead of around her.
And the jewellery didn’t just accessorise the look — it haunted it, in the most beautiful way. A peridot-and-gold choker sitting like a secret at the collarbone. A hair chain cascading from the ear into the hairline, catching the light every time she turned. Stacked antique-gold bangles, a haathphool draped like lace across her knuckles, a clutch embroidered to echo the lehenga petal for petal.
This wasn’t dressing up for a wedding. This was dressing like the moment mattered.
#JustSpreehIt

posts.caption:
#Givenchy Spring 1997 was #AlexanderMcqueens first collection after taking over from #JohnGalliano. It actually received mixed reactions, many felt McQueen was too young and too rebellious for a house built on traditional French elegance.
His appointment represented a huge shift in fashion, as younger designers began taking control of historic couture houses. McQueen himself later criticized the collection, but over time it became one of the most important moments in his career.
#Zendaya in @givenchy Spring 1997 and accompanying @philiptreacy headpiece, styled by @luxurylaw
posts.alt_text: #PierpaoloPiccioli just presented his first #HauteCouture collection for #Balenciaga. A return to the codes of this 100 year old couture house established by #CristobalBalenciaga
#PierpaoloPiccioli just presented his first #HauteCouture collection for #Balenciaga. A return to the codes of this 100 year old couture house established by #CristobalBalenciaga

posts.caption:
For his Fall 2026 #Schiaparelli Couture collection, #DanielRoseberry explained that he had deliberately travelled to #Barcelona in the hope that the city would inspire his new collection.
One of the collection’s key influences was the work of #AntoniGaudí and his distinctive architecture. Gaudí’s influence can be seen throughout several looks, particularly those inspired by the façade of #CasaBatlló and the sun mosaic at Park Güell.
Schiaprelli used intricate embroidery and embellishment, including real flowers that were fed sugar water to preserve their colour and flexibility before being carefully embroidered onto the garments. Echoing Gaudí’s iconic mosaic techniques and organic forms, transforming architectural details into wearable art.
posts.alt_text: #PierpaoloPiccioli just presented his first #HauteCouture collection for #Balenciaga. A return to the codes of this 100 year old couture house established by #CristobalBalenciaga

posts.caption:
Ahead of #JonathanAnderson’s second couture debut, step inside the salons of 30 Avenue Montaigne and rediscover the work of #ChristianDior in the 1950s, a period that defined an era of fashion and cemented #Paris as the capital of #HauteCouture.
#Dior became renowned for his ability to reinvent the female silhouette almost every season. Throughout the 1950s, he continued to develop the New Look with its tiny, corseted waist, rounded shoulders, and full calf-length skirts crafted from luxurious silks, satins, taffetas, and organza.
posts.alt_text: #PierpaoloPiccioli just presented his first #HauteCouture collection for #Balenciaga. A return to the codes of this 100 year old couture house established by #CristobalBalenciaga

posts.caption:
Founded in 1838, @charvet_official was one of the world’s first dedicated shirtmaker. It pioneered the concept of luxury bespoke shirting and has long been recognised as one of the most discreet and respected maisons.
A full circle moment, the shirts hold a special place in #GabrielleChanel’s own history. Her great love and inspiration, Arthur “Boy” Capel, was known to wear #Charvet shirts, and #Chanel famously drew on masculine tailoring to redefine women’s fashion.
For his debut @chanelofficial collection, #MatthieuBlazy continued his signature of elevating everyday essentials through exceptional craftsmanship and collaborated with Charvet on shirts, each finished with pearl buttons and Chanel’s signature chain sewn into the hem.
posts.alt_text: #PierpaoloPiccioli just presented his first #HauteCouture collection for #Balenciaga. A return to the codes of this 100 year old couture house established by #CristobalBalenciaga

posts.caption:
One of my favourite details from #Jacquemus’ sun-soaked show in Corsica was the series of headscarves that became part of the garments themselves. These looks embodied @simon_porte_jacquemus’ vision of Mediterranean dressing.
A close-fitting hood was tied at the nape of the neck before flowing down the back as a long panel of fabric, draping into a cowl seamlessly transforming into a dress or coat. It was a sculptural silhouette that feels both minimalist and dramatic, relying on fluid construction.
They also recall the practical headscarves traditionally worn by women across the #Mediterranean, the draped silhouettes of ancient #Greek and #Roman dress, and veils seen in religious imagery.
posts.alt_text: #PierpaoloPiccioli just presented his first #HauteCouture collection for #Balenciaga. A return to the codes of this 100 year old couture house established by #CristobalBalenciaga

posts.caption:
Justin Bieber for Issue 01: “The First Ever” Outlander Magazine In-Print!

posts.caption:
Travis Scott for Issue 01: “The First Ever” Outlander Magazine In-Print!
GANG, TRAVIS SCOTT IN HERMÈS FOR OUR FIRST EVER MAGAZINE COVER. THIS IS UNHEARD OF, AND BETTER YET? IT WAS FOR OUTLANDER MAGAZINE. I AM HUMBLED.
WOW, what a moment. My only wish in this life is that what I’ve spent the last 10 years building acts as a strong form of inspiration for your own creative careers - I came from no fashion background, I had no connections & I had no idea what I was doing, but, I did it anyway.
If you didn’t know by now, my name is @callum.mccaff , I founded Outlander - and I’m here to remind everyone that PRINT ISN’T DEAD.

posts.caption:
Fashion styling is a job. Not a hobby. Not a passion project. Not something people do on the side because they love clothes.
It is a profession that takes years to build. The eye. The contacts. The knowledge of how the industry actually works across every sector, every brief, every type of client. That does not come from loving fashion. It comes from years of showing up, doing the work and figuring it out mostly alone.
And yet it is still one of the most undervalued roles in the creative industry.
Stylists are asked to work for free. For credit. For exposure. For a rate that does not come close to reflecting the skill, the hours or the experience involved. And somewhere along the way the industry decided that was normal. That stylists should be grateful for the opportunity. That loving what you do means you should not expect to be paid properly for it.
We are past that.
Behind every iconic image, every red carpet moment, every campaign that stopped you mid-scroll, there was a stylist. Building the looks, interpreting the brief, managing the pressure, absorbing the chaos so nobody else had to. Often without a credit. Sometimes without a proper rate.
That changes when stylists know their worth, charge it without apology and stop accepting the jobs that treat their career like a favour.
➡️➡️ If you are a working fashion stylist who is ready to do exactly that, comment STUDIO and we will send you the free trial link to The Studio of Stylist Elixir. Rate guides. Contracts. Portfolio tools. Masterclasses every Monday. Everything you need to build a career that reflects what you are actually worth.
Save this. Send it to a stylist who needs to hear it.
posts.alt_text:
Jonathan Anderson just showed his first couture collection for Dior. If you do not understand why it mattered, this post is for you.

John Galliano was front row. The artisans were the opening act. The silhouettes came from ceramicist Magdalene Odundo.

If you are in Paris: the show space at Musée Rodin opens to the public from 28 January for one week. The new couture collection, archival Dior pieces, and the Odundo ceramics that inspired it. You can see it yourself.

This is not about liking or not liking a collection. This is about understanding what you are looking at.
The Studio of Stylist Elixir is our online membership. Guides. Templates. Live calls. A community of stylists who are learning and building together. Comment ‘studio’ for a free trial.

Save this. Share it with someone who needs it.

#dior #jonathananderson #stylistelixir

posts.caption:
I’ve been so busy daydreaming about this collection that I almost forgot to post it. Matthieu Blazy’s FW26 Haute Couture collection for Chanel transformed the Grand Palais into an enchanted storybook world for Gaby and the Beanstalk.
Inspired by a volume of fairy tales from Coco Chanel’s personal library, Blazy reimagined the house’s heritage through whimsical craftsmanship rather than nostalgia. The collection evolved from effortless daywear into ethereal evening looks, featuring sheer mousseline takes on the classic tweed suit, trompe l’œil textures, chain mail, and fluid pastel gowns.
The magic was in the details: buttons transformed from ugly ducklings into swans, heels became golden eggs, and miniature garden scenes were sculpted into the shoes themselves.
One thing about Matthieu Blazy—he knows how to tell a story.
#Chanel #MatthieuBlazy #HauteCouture
posts.alt_text:
Everyone say it with me: THANK YOU, PIERPAOLO!!!!! Pierpaolo Piccioli has officially begun a new era at Balenciaga, unveiling his highly anticipated haute couture debut, and I’m completely obsessed.

Stepping away from the conceptual irony that defined the previous era, Piccioli delivered a collection rooted in romance, emotion, and extraordinary craftsmanship. Rich color, fluid movement, sculptural silhouettes, and impeccable couture techniques reminded everyone that this label isn’t all about latex and leather…

The colors. The textures. The movement. The venue. The casting. Balenciaga feels romantic again, and I am so here for it.

Drop your thoughts.

#Balenciaga #PierpaoloPiccioli

Earlier this month, @heavenmayhem_ brought back its Holiday Supper Club in LA, this time in collaboration with @iliabeauty.
Set in the backyard of Spencer’s Spa, the night leaned into a relaxed, slightly undone kind of summer: a long table, natural light, and a crowd that felt more like friends than guests.
Dinner came courtesy of Slutty Cooking, with photography by Em Feretti and a soundtrack curated by Heaven Mayhem Radio—equal parts intimate and chaotic in the best way.
#HeavenMayhem #IliaBeauty #Spencers

posts.caption:
Celine might have just convinced me that silk scarves deserve a permanent spot in everyone’s wardrobe.
The house’s latest campaign feels like a study in movement, color, styling, and actually building a world around a product. From scarves draped as head coverings to windswept flags and even wrapped around a Dalmatian, every image finds a new way to make a familiar accessory feel unexpectedly modern.
Needless to say, my summer scarf obsession has officially begun.
#Celine

posts.caption:
Couture is often used as a catch-all term in fashion to describe opulent fashion, but authentic couture (called Haute Couture, which translates from “high sewing” in French) is much more complex. In France, Haute Couture isn’t just a type of collection, but a legally protected title regulated by the French Ministry of Industry and fiercely policed by the Chambre Syndicale de la Haute Couture. It’s a structural gauntlet so severe that only a permanent inner circle holds full sovereign status, while foreign powerhouses are relegated to “corresponding” members, and independent designers enter the calendar strictly by temporary guest invitations. And while primary shows take place twice a year in Paris, the couture ecosystem also extends to other foreign and non-syndicated designations like Alta Moda.
It’s a lot to keep up with, but I’ve gathered everything all in one place for you here: from the history of couture and how it saved thousands of garment workers from Nazi Germany, the essential legal conditions and vocabulary for syndicated and non-syndicated couture, what goes into producing a collection, and who’s buying couture—and why.
Read the latest on Substack: The Citizen’s Poste | posteposteposte.com
#FashionSubstack #HauteCouture #Schiaparelli #Dior #Chanel

posts.caption:
Schiaparelli is officially back, and Daniel Roseberry opened Paris Couture Week with one of the season’s most anticipated collections… but what else is new?
Titled The Abyss, the FW26 Haute Couture collection embraced an aquatic, surrealist vision, trading rigid couture formulas for the kind of fantasy that’s become synonymous with Roseberry’s tenure at Schiaparelli.
And he positioned the collection as a response to the growing influence of AI in fashion, arguing for the enduring value of human emotion, imagination, and extraordinary craftsmanship (not to mention attention to detail).
A very strong opening to couture week and another reminder that the future of fashion may be technological, but couture remains deeply human.
Comment your thoughts—and if you want to read more about the world of Haute Couture, check out our “Study” section on Substack.
#Schiaparelli #DanielRoseberry #HauteCouture
posts.alt_text:
I’ve been so busy daydreaming about this collection that I almost forgot to post it. Matthieu Blazy’s FW26 Haute Couture collection for Chanel transformed the Grand Palais into an enchanted storybook world for Gaby and the Beanstalk.

Inspired by a volume of fairy tales from Coco Chanel’s personal library, Blazy reimagined the house’s heritage through whimsical craftsmanship rather than nostalgia. The collection evolved from effortless daywear into ethereal evening looks, featuring sheer mousseline takes on the classic tweed suit, trompe l’œil textures, chain mail, and fluid pastel gowns.

The magic was in the details: buttons transformed from ugly ducklings into swans, heels became golden eggs, and miniature garden scenes were sculpted into the shoes themselves.

One thing about Matthieu Blazy—he knows how to tell a story.

#Chanel #MatthieuBlazy #HauteCouture

posts.caption:
Delhi is not a market Lululemon (@lululemon) needs to explain itself to. It has long been understood, just not officially stocked.
The Canadian brand’s first India store will open at DLF Promenade later this year through its franchise partnership with Tata CLiQ (@tatacliqfashion), converting years of airport hauls and global wardrobe fluency into local retail.
The sharper play is distribution: the Delhi store arrives alongside launches on Tata CLiQ Luxury and Tata CLiQ Fashion, making the debut physical in the capital but national online. The store will carry Lululemon’s technical apparel and lifestyle pieces across yoga, Pilates, running, training, tennis, golf, and everyday movement.
posts.alt_text:
Everyone is chasing new AI job titles. In India, the real shift is happening inside old roles.

Software engineers, educators, marketers, and reviewers are finding that the job title may have survived, but the job description has not. AI is now stitched into daily workflows, changing expectations faster than titles can keep up.

Then there is the invisible workforce: annotators, moderators, and trainers who keep the system running, but rarely get counted in the AI jobs boom.

The story of AI jobs in India is really a story of work being rewritten in real time.

Full story at the link in bio.
By Saumya Rastogi (@saumyaarastogi)

#AIJobs #WorkCulture #IndiaWorkforce #AIImpact #FutureOfJobs

posts.caption:
Long before gelato became a personality type and desserts started arriving in clean-label tubs, cassata ice cream had already perfected the drama.
Inspired by the Italian cassata dessert, it became something entirely its own in India: sponge cake, tutti-frutti, pista, strawberry, and synthetic colour, all stacked into one slice that felt fancier than it had any right to.
For many Indians, cassata ice cream was childhood luxury. Wedding buffets, old-school parlours, family dinners, freezer boxes opened for guests. A dessert that looked like occasion, even before anyone knew how to describe it.
Full story at the link in bio.
By Karishma Kuenzang (@kkuenzang)
#FoodHistory #Cassata #IndianDesserts #Nostalgia #Culture

posts.caption:
Rahul Mishra’s (@rahulmishra_7) couture is easiest to understand through the designer he has always tried to be: a storyteller who sees fashion as an ecosystem of labour, memory, craft, and belief.
That impulse has shaped his work for years, from his commitment to artisan communities to the way he has steadily expanded the language of Indian craft. Those who have watched Mishra move from Indian fashion weeks to winning the International Woolmark Prize and now showing at the Paris Haute Couture Week have also watched a designer grow increasingly confident in his own vocabulary.
That confidence sits at the centre of his latest couture collection, DEVI: The Eternal Muse. Mishra is no longer simply translating Indian history for an international room. He is excavating it. The collection experiments with trompe-l’œil surfaces that suggest sandstone, basalt, and bronze rather than fabric. What holds it together is the way Mishra makes cloth carry the weight of stone. Zardozi, dabka, crystals, stones, and bugle beads build depth and texture, turning embroidery into architecture and couture into moving sculpture.
And yet, DEVI is where Mishra’s defining strength begins to reveal its limits. The handwork is extraordinary, but the idea sometimes becomes too literal, too heavily loaded. The goddess, the muse, the artefact, the monument: the woman is asked to hold so much symbolism that she risks becoming less human than icon.
What the collection reveals is not whether Indian couture can hold its own in Paris. That argument is already dated. Mishra’s craft is never in question. What it needs now is risk: a willingness to let beauty become less obedient, less reverent, and perhaps more uncomfortable.
📸: Chloé Rasquinet (@lookwhatyoumademesee), Arina Prikhodko (@arineeel), Gourab Ganguli (@gourabganguli), Moral moda (@moralmoda_visionnaire), karlaotto (@karlaotto) ,silxstaryst, Mateusz Pielesz (@mateuszpielesz)

posts.caption:
The old celebrity deal was a face on a campaign. The new one is a face on the cap table. For years, Indian celebrities have helped consumer brands borrow trust, speed up discovery, and make products feel culturally inevitable. Kareena Kapoor Khan’s (@kareenakapoorkhan ) investment in Fizzy Goblet (@fizzygoblet) is best read against the financial value and earning power of her public image. In 2024, Kapoor Khan’s brand value was estimated at $79.5 million by Kroll.
Kapoor Khan’s move from endorsement to equity asks why that value should end at a campaign fee. Her earlier bets, from Pluckk to Quench Botanics, follow the same logic, revealing an understanding of the consumer economy orbiting her: the food women want to eat healthier, the skincare they are curious about, the festive fashion they want to be seen in.
With Fizzy Goblet, that equation becomes cleaner. The amount remains undisclosed, but the value of the deal is not limited to the cheque. It converts a familiar celebrity association into a growth signal at a moment when the brand is chasing scale, retail expansion, and sharper consumer recall. Kapoor Khan has already helped make Fizzy Goblet more visible and more legible to a consumer who wants Indian craft without the heaviness of heritage retail.
The investment reflects a broader shift in how influence is being monetised in India. Celebrity image is now being treated as an asset class, with equity as the more ambitious end of the deal.

posts.caption:
BL drama has moved from Asian fandoms to Indian screens, fan pages, and Instagram series.
With Romil and Jugal, Mujhe Rang De, and The Visitor, the genre is making queer romance more visible, more watchable, and harder to treat as a side plot. The audience is there too: Rajvi Jhala of bl_imagines says interest is strongest among 25–34-year-olds, followed closely by 18–25-year-olds. Several episodes of Mujhe Rang De have also crossed one million views.
But visibility is not the same as acceptance. In a country where queer rights remain fragile, BL raises a more thornier question: is this a cultural shift, or does queer love still feel safest on screen?
Read more by Anant at the link in bio.
#BLdrama #QueerRepresentation #IndianBL #BoyLoveGenre #TheEstablished

posts.caption:
Robert Wun has a rare gift. he doesn’t simply present a collection, he invites you into a completely different world.
for Child’s Play, childhood becomes the starting point, but this isn’t a nostalgic trip down memory lane. it’s a reflection on growing up, remembering, imagining, and seeing familiar things through a completely different lens.
#TFO #couture #robertwun
posts.alt_text:
sometimes a collection isn’t trying to give you all the answers.

alongside today’s couture show, Pierpaolo Piccioli shared a note thanking the atelier, describing the collection as “the result of a feeling” and “our own language.” it feels less like a manifesto and more like a conversation.

we’re still figuring out exactly where Piccioli wants to take Balenciaga. is he moving closer to Cristóbal Balenciaga’s couture purity? is he building something entirely his own? maybe that’s the point. for now, it feels like a house finding a new rhythm rather than rushing to define one.

#TFO #balenciaga #piccioli #fashion #couture

posts.caption:
some brands spend years trying to earn a place on the Paris couture calendar. Standing Ground arrived and made it look like they’ve been doing this since forever.
designer Michael Stewart’s debut collection was built on obsessive craftsmanship: sculptural corsetry developed with legendary corset-maker Mr. Pearl, thousands of beads threaded one by one to create structure instead of decoration, and draping inspired by Ireland’s ancient standing stones. and the finale was this insane Carrickmacross lace wedding dress that took 26 artisans over 4,000 hours to complete.
and that’s how you enter the couture calendar.
#TFO #StandingGround #pariscouture #couture #hautecouture

posts.caption:
after decades at the centre of music, fashion and street culture, the PUMA suede is proving that true icons will also be there.
the latest “never not suede” campaign follows a new generation of creatives through the streets of the city, celebrating the communities and cultures that have kept the silhouette relevant for decades. styled by Imruh Asha, photographed by Maxime Ballesteros and shot on film by filmmaker Natas, the campaign feels as timeless as the sneaker itself.
launching in black, bold orange and coffee brown, the suede classic returns on 9 July, with more collaborations and new interpretations set to follow throughout the year.
#TFO #PUMA #pumasuede #sneakers #fashion

posts.caption:
this week’s creator: @deemakeupart
the LA-based artist has worked with Chappell Roan, Doechii, Doja Cat and across editorial and beauty campaigns, building a signature that’s impossible to mistake. fearless colour, flawless execution and looks that somehow feel both futuristic and wearable.
bold colour, unexpected details and skin that somehow looks almost unreal. if you see her looks you know it’s her.
#TFO #makeupartist #editorialmakeup #beauty #fashionbeauty
posts.alt_text:
some brands make fragrance campaigns, Chanel makes full cinematic universes!

The Chase starring Jacob Elordi for Bleu De Chanel, directed by Alfonso Cuarón, feels less like a perfume ad and more like the kind of man your therapist specifically warned you about!

the tension, the tailoring, the blue lighting, the eye contact…

#TFO #CHANEL #BLEUDECHANEL

posts.caption:
the McQueen skull isn’t just one of fashion’s most recognisable motifs—it completely reshaped the house’s visual identity.
the latest episode of “Documenting McQueen” traces its journey from Lee McQueen’s 1990s collections to the now-iconic skull scarf launched in 2003, exploring how a single symbol evolved into a cultural phenomenon. along the way, it revisits unforgettable archive moments, including Kate Moss wearing the bespoke skull-print dress created for “The Black Show” in 2004.
if you love fashion history as much as new collections, this one’s definitely worth the watch.
#TFO #mcqueen #alexandermcqueen #fashionarchive #fashionhistory

posts.caption:
Hi, I’m Veronica 👋🏼 a sewing pattern designer based in Melbourne, Australia.
I create patterns, tools and resources that help you sew with understanding.
My work focuses on how fabrics behave, how garments are constructed, and how sewing connects us to the history of what we wear.
Sewing Patterns
My patterns are designed for versatility, wearability and fun. They include the Hazel, Freya, Odette, Audrey, Hera, Cordelia and the Sleeve Studio — each combining comfort with thoughtful design details. You’ll also find The Noelle, a free beginner-friendly pattern that introduces my approach to fit and construction.
The Paper Doll
A print-at-home paper doll designed to help you visualise and play with my sewing patterns. You can cut it out and colour it yourself, or use the pre-filled versions featuring my fabric prints and colour palettes. The set includes curvier model options and three skin tones to choose from.
The Conscious Consumer eBook
A practical guide to fabrics, sustainability and sewing with intention. It helps you understand fibre origins, weave structures and how to make long-lasting choices when you sew or buy clothes.
Digital Sewing Tools
Two paid calculators designed for precision:
• Shirring Calculator – works out fabric shrinkage and elastic thread length.
• GSM Calculator – estimates fabric weight and drape.
Available individually or as a discounted bundle.
My PDF sewing patterns are stocked digitally through @fabricgodmother , @spotlightstores , @thefoldline , @joans.finefabric and @thedrapersdaughter .
Fabric and Accessories
You can shop my fabric designs on @spoonflower and find my illustrated phone cases at @thedairy .
Everything I create is designed to help you sew with confidence, understanding and creativity.
For all enquires email us at hello@veronicatuckerthelabel.com
posts.alt_text:
This was a huge milestone for me because it taught me something I needed to learn. 

Working in the fashion industry taught me that what I physically produced and created was equivalent to my worth. 

Images and visuals are the primary language in the fashion industry, especially when communicating with manufacturing teams whose first language might not be english. 

So I didn’t put much value on my writing or my philosophical views around fashion and culture. It was always a dialogue that was happening inside me, something I didn’t think connected to my sewing work. 

But this year I’ve really leant into writing and finding my voice on Substack. 

I think I can get hung up on thinking I need to sound a certain way to be taken seriously, so it’s been a learning curve to find what my voice actually sounds like. 

I’m still learning and figuring it out but I’m really enjoying the dynamic of Substack and the space to write out my thoughts when they don’t fit into an Instagram carousel. 

So here are my articles since I started in March. 

Read the full articles via the link in my bio 🔗

First slide image is my Cordelia sewing pattern with my Hera sleeve sewing pattern mixed together 🥰 shop via the link in my bio

posts.caption:
Rembrandt once gave us instructions on how to view his work….”Don’t stand too close.” he said.
This week was session 3 of @readingsbooks Big Book Club on The Odyssey and we learned a little history about oral poetry.
Dr Tom Hercules Davies, a comparative philologist at @unimelb actually sung a few lines from the poem to help us understand the important of the musicality of the text.
Genuinely, it was so fascinating!
Read the full essay via the link on my bio
In partnership with @readingsbooks

More length though could mean arranging that shoulder to sit higher OR attaching to the back bodice to maintaining the angle of the gather. Just my thoughts. I love the silhouette of the dress in general.

posts.caption:
I didn’t know I could love Madame Grès any more, but here we are.
As much as I love her designs and her meticulous work (and they are mind flowingly beautiful), I would give anything to go to her atelier when she was working, sit down and talk to her…that’s if she even would have let me.
There’s something about her that I connect with. Maybe it was her penchant for expensive fabrics and her lack of budgeting. Maybe it was her ability to stand up for herself when everything around her was trying to tear her down. Or maybe it was her steadfast attitude towards her work.
I also love that her friends, Cristobal Balenciaga, Yves Saint Laurent and Pierre Cardin, banded together helped her out when she was struggling financially. How sweet is that?
All I know was that despite having a sad end, she lived a fascinating life.
Swipe to learn more about this fascinating womann and watch the full YouTube video about her life now. I also drape on the mannequin and have a bit of a moment of connection with her after doing so.

A practical guide to fabrics, sustainability and sewing with intention. It helps you understand fibre origins, weave structures and how to make long-lasting choices when you sew or buy clothes.

Digital Sewing Tools
Two paid calculators designed for precision:
• Shirring Calculator – works out fabric shrinkage and elastic thread length.
• GSM Calculator – estimates fabric weight and drape.
Available individually or as a discounted bundle.

Stockists
My PDF sewing patterns are stocked digitally through @fabricgodmother , @spotlightstores , @thefoldline , @joans.finefabric and @thedrapersdaughter .

Fabric and Accessories
You can shop my fabric designs on @spoonflower and find my illustrated phone cases at @thedairy .

Everything I create is designed to help you sew with confidence, understanding and creativity.

For all enquires email us at hello@veronicatuckerthelabel.com

posts.alt_text:
Few actors inspire as much online discourse as Alia Bhatt. Conversations around nepotism, privilege and public perception have increasingly eclipsed those about her performances, turning her into one of Bollywood’s most scrutinised and trolled stars. A week into the release of her latest spy thriller _Alpha_, we investigate the incense and vitriol and why it feels so disproportionate. Link in bio.

🖋️ @shrutijn6891 

@aliaabhatt 
#thevofashion #aliabhatt

posts.caption:
It’s fascinating how people find it easier to tear down a woman succeeding in a male-dominated industry than hold accountable men with documented histories of violence against women or multiple sexual assault allegations that somehow disappear from public discourse.
But yes, let’s direct all that outrage at Alia Bhatt because she’s successful.
And before anyone says, “We’re only criticizing her acting,” criticism of someone’s work is one thing. The relentless abuse, misogyny, death threats, and rape threats she receives are something else entirely. Most people wouldn’t last a day dealing with that level of hate.
posts.alt_text: The Atlas Of Affluence, the definitive white paper on the Rise of Indian Luxury and what it means across categories, culture, craft and new models of aspirations. Launching today.

posts.caption: Temple sculptures belong to everyone.... sounds very comforting. History otherwise says that they didn't even belong to the people who made them. Temple sculptures were created by hereditary artisan communities such as the Vishwakarmas in Hindu caste society. Brahmin priest had to conduct rituals to purify the sculpture from the impure touch of the lower caste sculptor - the sculpture then became deity. These sculptors although recorded occasionally, never got much credit for the art while ruling-class kings, patrons, and dynasties were memorialised through inscriptions and history. That pattern never disappeared. Today, those same sculptures are reborn through the embroidery and textile skills preserved and passed on by hereditary artisan communities, yet the authorship, prestige, media attention, and profits flow to designers, brands, and other elite cultural curators instead of the people whose labour and knowledge make the work possible. Indian couture lacks big spectacle or vision once all that embroidery & craft is stripped away. The craft is visible. It's the craftsperson who is invisible. They were invisible back when they made those sculptures in temples or now when they embroided it on textiles. This is the cultural appropriation & credit theft upper caste Indians will be dead silent about.

posts.caption: This is deeply unserious collection with a very lazy literal take on India's sculpture heritage. It's the kind of stuff you see in fashion school grad collections or costume design for events and functions or for theater. For such tacky literal motif inspiration to be done and applauded is not very creative or intelligent. The clothes DO NOT look high fashion or couture. And so much of it looks like a mockery of the theme. Not to forget the strangeness of black models being used to represent grey black stone sculpture. Really? This stuff looks like shoot out of filmfare in the 90s.

posts.caption:
Even if you know nothing about Masaba Gupta (@masabagupta), at the very least you know that she’s famous. Some people know her simply as Masaba, founder of the fashion label House of Masaba (@houseofmasaba) that, for the last 17 years, has been making playfully printed contemporary Indian clothing. Others know her as the star of the Netflix show ‘Masaba Masaba’. To the chronically online, she’s the powerful influencer, the person brands go to when they want a cool, articulate personality to represent them. The makeup-obsessed know her via LoveChild by Masaba (@lovechildbymasaba), the beauty brand she started in 2021, which has taken her obsession with makeup and turned it into products built specifically around Indian skin tones.
Yet, as she continues to do it all, there is a change underway. As we walk out of her office, Masaba casually drops a piece of news. “I’m moving away from ready-to-wear entirely. I think spring/summer 2026 is the last one we’ll do. The focus is going to be on occasion and bridalwear.” It’s a pretty big declaration, and a timely one, considering that later this month Masaba will be a part of the FDCI Hyundai India Couture Week in New Delhi. Right now, it feels like Masaba is moving into a new era, one where she’s more of a founder and businesswoman than a niche fashion designer.
At the link in bio, @butoolbegum spends the day in the company of the enterprising designer, business owner, and new mom.

posts.alt_text:
Fatherhood is a new role for the actor who first got noticed for his performance in ‘Masaan’ (2015). In fact, it seems this off-camera Vicky Kaushal (@vickykaushal09) is perhaps closer to Deepak Chaudhary, the character he played in the film. A sensitive, ambitious young man coming to terms with the world around him. That character, Kaushal says, changed him. But not for the obvious reasons.

“Until then success, to me, was where you reached [in life]. It was never about where you started from,” he explains. “That character, starting from the crematorium ghat in Benaras and going on to work as an engineer in the Railways in Allahabad [now Prayagraj]... He had a far bigger life arc than I’ve covered in my life. Till today,” he says, tapping the arm rest for emphasis. “After that I started having so much empathy for those around me, especially those from far less privileged backgrounds, because I don’t know where they have started from.”

@travellerprasad in conversation with the Bollywood actor at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
Few artists in India’s independent music scene resist being bracketed quite like Chaar Diwaari (@chaardiwaari). His work dissolves the boundaries between rap, indie, alternative rock, Bollywood orchestration, and cinematic storytelling, creating immersive worlds where albums unfold like films. In February, he released 'Parvana', an EP that unfolds like a psychological narrative where every song, visual, and recurring character forms part of a larger world.
The genre-hopping artist is also bridging generations with a roster of collaborations, which have seen the likes of legendary artists like Sonu Nigam (@sonunigamofficial) and Indian Ocean (@indianoceanmusic) to younger talents like Gini and Sanjith Hegde. His recent release ‘Iss Tarah’ with Nigam—an orchestral ballad layered over contemporary production—feels less like a novelty collaboration and more like a meeting point between two eras of Indian music. It’s this willingness to embrace contradiction—to blend his inherent oddness with a refreshing sense of realism and vulnerability—that has made him one of Gen Z’s most compelling cult artists.
At the link in bio, @tanya.91 chats with the 23-year-old from Delhi about '80s music, internet addiction, his hybrid-Indian personal style, and more.

posts.caption:
Since it's Wednesday, here's a little more pink to add to your 'fit 🩰
If you’ve been staying up late to watch the 2026 FIFA World Cup (@fifaworldcup), you’ve probably already clocked the striking pink cleats that the majority of athletes are wearing. The Barbie-ready hue has become the unofficial colour of the season. What made this striking colour the unexpected uniform of the season? One that unites players of all teams, be it Kylian Mbappé, Erling Haaland or Harry Kane. It’s a colour that’s guaranteed to stand out against the green of the pitch, increasing visibility and drawing attention to their products. In addition to practicality, pink is a real mood booster.
Luckily this obsession with fuchsia shoes has extended beyond the world of sports and into fashion too. At the link in bio, check @diyabakhle's full list to get into the football spirit without having to choose a team.
Image courtesy: @gettyimages
@alo @veja @newbalance @asics @autryglobal @chloe

posts.alt_text:
A summer well spent might look like this: lounging in the sun, a few more cocktails than usual, a late-night gelato, and an even later-night dancing session. And though this certainly sounds great—and can be, in moderation—your lymphatic system takes a hit. The first, and most viral, solution? A lymphatic drainage massage. However, there are plenty of other things you can do to help your lymphatic system without a professional. At the link in bio, learn more from massage therapist @Tehani_Leprieur as she breaks down some of her top recommendations.
Photographed by Angelo Penetta, Vogue, June 2015

posts.caption: One of 2026’s biggest makeup trends? The blurred lip—and @NaomiWatts's glam for @Dior's fall 2026 couture show proves the technique is still going strong for summer. “With Naomi in black and white, adding that pop of red on the lips felt like the perfect choice, timeless, chic, and unmistakably French,” shares makeup artist Georgie Eisdell. At the link in bio, discover how Watts and Eisdell put their own spin on the trend. Photo and video: @georgieeisdellmakeup

posts.caption:
Are you doing enough cardio? The metric that measures cardio effectiveness, VO₂ max, may sound like something out of chemistry class, but it's actually an important component of living a long, healthy life. Longevity expert @DrVicenteMera points out that to get the most out of physical exercise, “you need to work on strength, cardio, and stretching without prioritizing one over the other.” Tap the link in bio to learn more about the direct link between VO₂ max and longevity, and how this could impact your workout routine.
Photographed by Steven Klein, Vogue, September 2003

posts.caption:
For their first Vogue India cover together, Padmanabh Singh (@pachojaipur) and Gauravi Kumari (@gauravikumari) turn a day in Singapore into a sibling sport. Born one year apart, they grew up like twins with very different operating systems. Gauravi is “the planner, the pseudo-mom”, while Padmanabh continues to baffle her with spontaneous travel plans and a living-out-of-a-suitcase lifestyle.
Read the July-August cover story at the link in bio.

On Padmanabh: Jacket, trousers; both HERMÈS (@hermes). Panthère de Cartier watch, Clash de Cartier bracelet; both CARTIER (@cartier).

posts.caption:
Alongside Manish Malhotra’s (@manishmalhotraworld) first couture collection on the Paris stage came the unveiling of his newest high jewellery line, adding another layer to the house’s evolving identity. Silhouettes embraced exaggerated volume, while oxblood and champagne emerged as the collection’s defining palette. Familiar faces including Neelam Gill and Kirandeep Chahal walked the runway and the sari was reimagined through sculptural drapes and richly embellished couture. Swipe through for the standout looks from the debut, highly inspired by the designer’s mother.
Image credits: @gettyimages
#VogueFashion #ParisFashionWeek #ManishMalhotra

posts.caption: Size inclusivity in menswear remains largely static across #MilanFashionWeek and #ParisFashionWeek, with this season also welcoming more polarized body ideals. Alongside the continued dominance of straight-size casting, there was a sharp focus on extreme physiques, from "protein chic" and hyper-muscular builds shaped by looksmaxxing, to the return of ultra-slim "slender man" silhouettes. We found that of the thousands of looks presented across Milan and Paris SS27 shows, only 0.2% were shown on plus-size models, remaining unchanged since FW26. Tap the link in bio for a break down of the full report.
posts.alt_text: Luxury brands are increasingly turning to immersive dining experiences to engage consumers, build community, and deepen emotional connections. From fashionista food-fluencers and bespoke culinary art to in-the-know restaurant collabs, gastronomy is becoming an extension of brand storytelling and a strategic marketing tool. As luxury shifts from owning products to experiencing the brand, senior reporter @JosephDBobowicz asks: is niche dining fashion’s next frontier? Tap the link in bio to find out more.

posts.caption:
Chaos is something @GracieAbrams has grown accustomed to. Eight years ago, she was a first-year student at Barnard with a cult fandom she’d fostered through Instagram; soon she was signed to Interscope, then on her first tour, then opening for Olivia Rodrigo and Taylor Swift. By the end of 2024, Abrams was a Grammy-nominated pop star filling arenas.
She finally took a breather last summer. “It’s not until you stop that you realize ‘Oh, I haven’t seen my friends in a long time. I haven’t cooked a meal in a long time,’” Abrams says. “The sleep schedule becomes regular for the first time in, like, four years.”
Now 26, she’s shifting into a new gear. Abrams’s third studio album, “Daughter from Hell,” out in July, sounds like her—earnest, introspective, folk-informed—but the production is more lush (“It feels like me in progress,” she says); she’s also in a happy relationship and making her first film, “Please,” with the filmmaker Halina Reijn.
For Vogue’s Summer 2026 issue, Brittany Spanos meets Gracie Abrams in Los Angeles and New York to talk about growing up, slowing down, falling in love, and where she’d like to go next. Tap the link in our bio to read the full profile.
Photographed by @larissahofmann, Written by @brittanyspanos, Styled by @jordenbickham, Hair by @tamastuzes, Makeup by @emikaneko, Manicure by @1.800.nailme, Tailoring by Irina Tshartaryan for Susie’s Custom Designs, Produced by @hyperion.la, Set Design by  @patienceharding. Shot on location @lucasmuseum. Vogue, Summer 2026.

posts.caption: Within the world of Grand Slams, Wimbledon is the creme de la creme. And if you’re lucky enough to snag a ticket, an outfit worthy of the elegant environs is in order. Looking to Wimbledon regulars like Sienna Miller, Alexa Chung, and Catherine, Princess of Wales for inspiration—and pulling from the season’s most coveted new shopping arrivals—shop five winning outfits to wear to Wimbledon and beyond at the link in our bio.
posts.alt_text: When it comes to heatwave dressing in the city, the street style set has decreed that the only way to wear your favorite jeans is with flip-flops. They can lighten up even the most structured looks, whether you’re wearing a pair of affordable Havaianas or Phoebe Philo’s latest bottle green flip-flops. As temperatures soar, we’ve rounded up 5 flip-flop and denim looks to set you up perfectly for heatwave dressing in the city. Shop the looks at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
Having the courage to step into the unknown—that is the commanding trait of our 'Audacity' issue. It is something our cover star @chua.tanya has never shied away from, even at the most tumultuous point of her career.
With little to no support for her musical direction back then, the esteemed Singaporean singer-songwriter was determined to escape to Taiwan with only three months worth of rent in her bank account. "I had just won a Golden Melody Award, but it’s not like that helps pay the bills. I let myself disintegrate entirely to find myself again. It was a turning point for me because it allowed me to ponder this question of why we are so afraid of failure," she reminisces.
For #VogueSingapore's July/Aug 'Audacity' issue, Chua lays herself bare, sharing with us the mettle behind her musical vision, her boundless curiosity for life and trying out new things. Read the full story now in the link in bio and preorder a copy, out on newsstands on 22 July.

posts.alt_text:
Curiousity never killed the cat. So at 51, @chua.tanya is still doing hardcore gymnastics; an indisputable reflection of what has always been a lifelong resolve—to always try the things that scare you. 

"I’m doing backflips and handstands at an age where most people would be terrified to try. But if you’re constantly fearful of failure, nothing is going to get done. You’ll never do anything interesting or out of the ordinary," our 'Audacity' cover star proclaims. 

In the link in bio, the Singaporean singer-songwriter's narrative may read mercurial yet there was always a steadfast nerve behind it all: be it back when she debuted with her English album in 1997, when she wanted to do a tour dedicated to her less recognised tracks or even when she decided to take up gymnastics later in life. 

The July/Aug 'Audacity' issue is available for pre-order, and will hit newsstands on 22 July.

posts.caption: To brew a cup of tea—it is no simple task. An age-old practice that has its origins in Yunnan, China, it only makes sense that one of the modern world’s tea leaders first took root there too. @chagee.sg's reputed roster of teas has always been a playground for diverse flavour profiles—their latest homage to the age-old tradition comes in the shape of a Signature Tea Box Series: five carefully sourced, premium varieties of tea bags that offer nuance in each sip and every steep—thoughtfully created so you can enjoy them from the comfort of your own abode. Read more in the link in bio. #VogueSingapore

posts.caption:
In the conversation about inclusive beauty, accessibility often gets mentioned. But going beyond packaging, truly accessible beauty takes many other factors into consideration.
Accessibility is a much debated word within the lexicon of “inclusive” beauty. Beyond packaging, beauty that is truly accessible can be misconstrued—not only accommodating for the physically disabled, but those with chronic illnesses and neurodivergents who are also part of the conversation. When @rarebeauty first launched, for example, its bulbous packaging became a point of contention—until its accessibility purposes were unveiled. Following @SelenaGomez’s lupus and arthritis diagnoses, designing products with such elements enabled her to use them without too much strain.
In conversation with licensed occupational therapist Kailey Waskall (@occupationbeauty), #VogueSingapore’s beauty writer @ailouest dives into the nitty-gritty of “accessible” beauty, the brands to watch out for, and the current gaps in the market. #VogueSingapore

posts.caption:
From @Madonna’s pink bridal veil for her surprise @YSL afterparty rave to @KatseyeWorld’s #Coachella performance and @Beyonce’s Cowboy Carter tour, @AgroStudio_ have become the designer duo behind some of pop’s most memorable wardrobes.
Founded by @anguxx and @geoxby, at the heart of Agro Studio lies an instinctive understanding of the body—creating pieces that transcend aesthetics, identities and silhouettes. Whether working with Chantilly lace, Mongolian fur or crystal corsetry, the duo’s technical fluency allows them to craft a distinct visual language for every wearer.
Alongside an exclusive behind-the-scenes video from their fall/winter 2026 collection ‘The Wanderer,’ the designer duo speaks with #VogueSingapore’s digital writer @agnesmshu about building Agro Studio, the influence of commercial work on their creative language, and taking ego out of the process. Read the full interview in the link in bio. #VogueSingapore

posts.caption:
: A strong marriage isn't built on grand gestures alone—it's built on consistent connection.
Sometimes, the biggest intimacy mistakes aren't intentional. From assuming your partner knows how you feel to letting busy schedules replace quality time, these small habits can quietly create distance over time.
Healthy intimacy grows through communication, appreciation, trust, emotional presence, and making your partner feel loved every single day. ❤️
💾 Save this post as a reminder.
💬 Which relationship habit do you think is the most important after marriage?
#MarriageTips #RelationshipGoals #ModernMarriage #MarriedLife #GroomTips #Newlyweds #HealthyRelationships #EmotionalIntimacy #CoupleGoals #LoveAndMarriage #WeddingPlanning #IndianWedding #GroomToBe #RelationshipAdvice #WeddingAffair #WeddingAffairOfficial #WeddingAffairMagazine
posts.alt_text:
: First Night Myths: Busted!

From the myth that "everything has to happen on the first night" to the belief that "it has to be perfect," it's time to leave unrealistic expectations behind. Every couple's experience is unique, and the best first night is the one that feels right for both of you.

💕 Remember:
✔️ Go at your own pace.
✔️ Communicate openly.
✔️ Respect each other's comfort and boundaries.
✔️ Focus on connection, not perfection.

❤️ Save this post for every bride and groom-to-be, and share it with a couple who needs this reminder!

📲 Follow @weddingaffairofficial for real relationship insights & modern love inspiration 💎
